recent_calls.summary: The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and no further interaction from the user before the call ended.

Brieann called to schedule a heating service appointment for a thermostat issue and provided her tenant Tangia Randall's contact information for scheduling. The agent confirmed the details and noted that the team would contact the tenant directly to arrange the appointment. Brieann declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the call experience.

The user called to follow up on a recent AC service related to an insurance claim for a compressor repair. The agent collected the user's details and scheduled a callback before 10 AM the next day to provide an update. The user declined information about maintenance programs and requested to speak with a live agent, after which the call was transferred.

The user called asking to speak with Shannon. The agent, Sophie, explained she could not transfer calls directly but attempted to connect the user to a live agent who could assist. The call was successfully transferred to another agent.
The user called back and the agent attempted to assist but quickly transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call lasted 16 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called back and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

John called to schedule a technician to check his central air conditioning before turning it on for the season. He was flexible with the appointment time and was provisionally booked for April 21 at 2 PM. He also requested that the technician who visited on September 18 not be sent again, and the agent confirmed this note and will pass it to the team.

The user called to request an invoice for a recent payment made to Rich. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to discuss a partnership opportunity with Budget, representing Assurant Home Warranty's expansion into home service repairs. The agent confirmed the user's contact details and assured that the appropriate team would follow up soon. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed a successful call transfer.

The user, Cody Meyer, called to schedule an appointment for ductwork inspection and AC repair at their home. The agent successfully booked a provisional appointment for April 21st between noon and 2 PM and collected the user's contact details. The user was satisfied with the clear communication and rated the experience highly.
The user called in response to a missed call from Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The user called in regarding a previous call about an interview. The agent acknowledged the user's concern and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to request an earlier appointment for urgent cooling service under their home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them soon. The call ended with the user being transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The agent attempted to assist the user by requesting their service address ZIP code, but the user declined to provide location details and requested to speak with a live operator. The agent then transferred the call to a live agent as requested.
The user called to get in touch with Amy, but the agent asked for the service address ZIP code to direct the request appropriately. The call ended shortly after without further information exchanged.
The user called and requested to speak with the service department. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate department.

The user, Jeff Hamilton, called to schedule a replacement for a gas water heater at his home in Wentzville, Missouri. The agent provided available appointment slots for April 21st, and the user agreed to one of the suggested times. The call concluded with the booking request confirmed.

The user called to confirm if a technician could service an AC unit sooner than the two-week timeframe initially given. The agent documented the request and informed the user that the team would follow up with availability. The user provided feedback rating the experience as average and requested faster response times.

Kristen called to report a leak around the drain hose after a technician's visit and requested the technician to return as soon as possible. The agent confirmed the contact details and service address, assured Kristen that the team would be notified immediately, and ended the call after confirming no further assistance was needed.

The user called to request the QMID manufacturer ID code for their newly installed furnace and AC unit to complete their tax filing before the deadline. The agent acknowledged the urgency, assured the user the request would be prioritized, and confirmed the user would be contacted by phone or email with the information. The call ended with the user satisfied that their request was being handled promptly.
The user called back and the agent, Sophie, promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.

The user requested to be transferred back to a specific agent who had availability on the twenty-ninth. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the requested agent.

Anthony called to schedule an urgent appointment for his malfunctioning AC unit. The agent provided available time slots, and Anthony selected April 22nd at 10 AM. The appointment was provisionally booked, but Anthony had a billing question about the dispatch fee, so the call was transferred to the billing team.
The user called and requested to speak to the manager. The agent attempted to transfer the call to a live agent who could assist, and the transfer was successful.

Debbie from American Home Shield called to provide details about a dispatch for an air conditioning repair involving a gas union and previous installation errors. The agent confirmed the updated service address and callback number, and Debbie confirmed the information was correct. The call ended with Debbie providing positive feedback and no further requests.

Angela Bursak called to report an air conditioning issue where the unit is not cooling and the pipe outside is freezing. She scheduled a service appointment for Thursday afternoon and provided her home warranty dispatch number and contact details. The agent confirmed the information and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact her shortly.
The user requested to be connected to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to schedule an appointment for a cooling issue under a home warranty. The agent gathered information about the problem and confirmed it was not an emergency. The user did not provide a dispatch number but expressed openness to scheduling.
The user requested to be connected to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent. The initial agent facilitated the transfer promptly without any issues.

The user called to set up a service appointment for a cooling issue under their home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that the request would be passed to the team for follow-up. The user confirmed the summary but indicated a change was needed before the call ended.

The user called to set an appointment for their home warranty. The agent informed the user that such appointments are handled differently and that the request would be passed to the appropriate team for follow-up. The call ended after this exchange.

The user expressed dislike for AI during the call, and the agent responded by transferring the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another number.

Timothy Smith called to schedule a cooling service appointment under his home warranty. The agent confirmed the details and offered available time slots on April 21st, which Timothy accepted for 12 PM. The call ended with the appointment successfully booked.

Ashley from American Wholesale called to inquire about heating service in Silex for an AC unit issue. The agent confirmed service availability, collected detailed information, and offered appointment slots, but the user declined the proposed times and requested alternative dates. The call ended with the agent noting the user's preferred dates for follow-up.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for an AC unit that isn't turning on under an American Home Shield home warranty but did not have the dispatch number. The agent took the details and assured the user that the team would follow up to confirm the appointment. The user rated the experience positively but expressed a dislike for AI.

Karen called to report that her air conditioning system is running but not cooling, with the thermostat set to 60 degrees while the house remains at 78 degrees. She requested service around 5 PM the same day, and the agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her soon. Karen expressed interest in receiving information about free maintenance programs if available.
The call was a brief interaction where the agent greeted the user and attempted to assist, but the user only confirmed if the agent could hear them before the call ended abruptly.

The user called to follow up on a case involving a warranty claim related to a service visit by Devon. The agent attempted to assist but the user requested a live agent, leading to the call being transferred to another representative.

The user called to request an electrician to come out, specifying it was not an emergency but they needed to speak with a live person. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Tyanna Elam called to schedule a service appointment for her central air unit, which is not working. The agent collected her contact details and confirmed the soonest available appointment. Tyanna expressed interest in home maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the communication during the call.

Angela from American Home Shield called Budget to clarify a work order related to air conditioning issues. She requested a live agent to discuss the possibility of a return visit for the member, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.

The user called to inquire about the delay and cost of replacement caps and Freon for their central air conditioning, expressing confusion about charges despite coverage by American Home Shield. The agent acknowledged the concerns and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance. The call ended with the user being transferred for more specialized help.

Joe Galvin called to clarify how to send payment upfront for a furnace replacement under his American Home Shield home warranty. He expressed difficulty with computers and was transferred to the billing team for assistance. The agent confirmed the service details and arranged the transfer to resolve the payment issue.

The user, Eve from American Home Shield, called to follow up on a dispatch regarding the installation of two coils for an air conditioning system. The agent confirmed the dispatch details, updated the contact phone number, and assured that the team would follow up as soon as possible. The call ended with the user satisfied with the updated information.

The user called to inquire about appliance repair services, specifically for a washer with a broken belt. The agent informed the user that they do not service household appliances but focus on heating, cooling, plumbing, and indoor air quality systems. The user declined further assistance and ended the call.

The user called to schedule a service for a cooling system issue likely caused by a bad capacitor on the blower fan, through American Home Shield. The agent collected all necessary details including the service address and user information, and confirmed the summary with the user. The user agreed to receive information about maintenance programs and declined to provide feedback.

Carlos called to inquire about a missed air conditioning service appointment at his house and requested to be transferred to Jason, the contractor handling the job. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to Jason. No emergency was reported, and the user had already spoken to someone previously.
